subtitle: Outer hip tightness radiating down the thigh

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: TFL tightness is the upstream cause of IT band knee pain

mild.description: Outer hip or upper thigh tightness, feels like a tight band
mild.can_run_note: Yes — foam roll TFL before and after runs
why: TFL overuse compensating for weak glute medius
Prolonged sitting in hip flexion shortening the TFL
Cambered road running loading the outer hip asymmetrically
relief: Foam roll TFL — outer quad between hip and knee, not the IT band itself
Side-lying quad stretch reaching back for the foot
prevention: Glute medius strengthening: clamshells and lateral band walks
Avoid always running on the same side of a cambered road
Single-leg squat with knee tracking over second toe
Keep TFL mobile with regular foam rolling

moderate.description: Persistent outer hip and thigh tightness, pulling sensation during runs
moderate.can_run_note: Flat routes only — reduce pace and distance by 30%
why: Chronic TFL overactivation from sustained glute weakness
IT band tension high enough to refer into the outer knee
Hip internal rotation pattern increasing TFL load
relief: Daily TFL foam rolling and hip flexor stretching
Reduce weekly mileage by 30% for 2 weeks
Dry needling to TFL trigger points — physio can advise
prevention: Address glute medius weakness as the root cause
Hip abductor strength programme 3× per week
Gait analysis to identify hip drop or crossover pattern

severe.description: IT band pain extending from hip to knee, cannot run
severe.can_run_note: Rest — address upstream cause before returning
why: Severe IT band syndrome with both hip and knee involvement
Chronic TFL trigger points requiring hands-on treatment
Underlying hip biomechanics fault driving the entire chain
relief: Physiotherapy for manual therapy and biomechanical assessment
prevention: Full kinetic chain assessment before return to running
Hip strength base must be established before mileage builds

see_physio_if: Pain extends from hip to knee
No improvement after 2 weeks of foam rolling and strengthening
Pain present on both sides simultaneously
